task: A few people keep asking me to come out after dark, and I’m trying to decide what to say back without turning it into a whole argument. What’s a simple way to handle the back-and-forth if I already have something else going on?

turns.agent_response:
Keep it short and firm so it doesn’t turn into a debate.

You can say:

**“I can’t do after dark. If you want to meet, it has to be before then. I’m only doing one plan, so let me know by [time].”**

Or even simpler:

**“I’m already set for tonight, so I’m not changing plans. If it works before dark, cool. If not, another time.”**

If you want, I can help you turn that into a text that sounds more casual, more direct, or more polite.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s fine. But make it sound less like a whole speech. More like: “Can’t do after dark. If it’s before then, cool. If not, another time.”

Need a couple versions — like one for friends, one for somebody being pushy.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here are two short versions:

**For friends:**  
“Can’t do after dark. If it’s before then, cool. If not, another time.”

**For somebody being pushy:**  
“Not doing after dark. Before then works, otherwise another time.”

If you want, I can make them even more casual or more blunt.
